name: Lemon Bars-Low Fat
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Sugar (divided into 1/2 cup and 1/2 cup)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Position rack in the lower third of the oven.
2. Spray a baking pan lightly with vegetable oil spray.
3. Make the crust: In a medium mixing bowl, beat the butter until creamy. Add 1/3 cup sugar and beat at high speed for about one minute or until the mixture loses its crumbly texture and begins to form a mass. Beat in the egg yolk, yogurt, and vanilla. Gradually add the dry ingredients (flour, cornmeal, salt, baking soda) and beat on low speed just until combined.
4. Scrape the bowl and beater clean. Knead the mixture briefly with your hands to mix thoroughly.
5. Press the dough evenly into the prepared pan. Prick all over with a skewer or fork. If the pan is lightweight, place it on a baking sheet.
6. Bake until brown on top, about 20-25 minutes.
7. Make the topping: In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs and egg white with the remaining sugar until combined. Whisk in the lemon juice and zest. Whisk in the additional 1/4 cup flour.
8. When the crust is brown, reduce the oven temperature to 300°F (150°C). Pour the topping over the hot crust.
9. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping barely jiggles in the center when you shake the pan gently back and forth.
10. Cool on a rack. Chill before cutting into squares.
Serve cold or at room temperature, dusted with powdered sugar. They last a couple of days but are most delicious on the day they are baked. Do not freeze.
